task_completion.description: Create three new statuses and configure the workflow transitions for the Member role on Feature type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Administration > Statuses page showing 'Ready for QA', 'QA Passed', and 'Staging Deployed', OR the Administration > Workflows page showing a matrix with checkboxes enabled for transitions: In progress -> Ready for QA -> QA Passed -> Staging Deployed -> Closed.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Target Release' list custom field with specific version options and enable it for Feature and Bug types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Administration > Custom fields page showing 'Target Release' with format 'List', or a configuration screen showing the options 'v2.0.0-alpha', 'v2.1.0-beta', and 'v3.0.0' enabled for Feature and Bug types.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Release Coordination' project and add Alice, Bob, and Carol as Members.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Release Coordination' project overview or Members page showing alice.johnson, bob.smith, and carol.williams assigned the 'Member' role.

task_completion.description: Create three Feature work packages with specific assignees, statuses, and Target Release values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Work packages list in 'Release Coordination' showing three Features: 'Release v2.0.0...' (assigned to Alice, In progress, v2.0.0-alpha), 'Release v2.1.0...' (assigned to Carol, v2.1.0-beta), and 'Release v3.0.0...' (assigned to Bob, v3.0.0).

task_completion.description: Create a status-based board named 'Release Pipeline' with columns for the complete workflow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Boards module showing a board named 'Release Pipeline' with distinct columns for 'New', 'In progress', 'Ready for QA', 'QA Passed', 'Staging Deployed', and 'Closed'.

task_completion.description: Create a wiki page titled 'Release Management Process' documenting the workflow, candidates, and QA procedures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wiki module in the 'Release Coordination' project showing a page titled 'Release Management Process' containing text about the workflow, release candidates, owners, and QA sign-off.

integrity.description: The agent must use the OpenProject web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the OpenProject web UI in a browser, not using curl, API scripts, or rails console commands in the terminal to create the entities.

integrity.description: The agent must interact with the actual OpenProject instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenProject interface at localhost:8080, not a text editor drafting the answers or a mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent must not manipulate the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show PostgreSQL (`psql`) or `rails console` commands being used to insert statuses, custom fields, or work packages directly into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenProject 15 workflow configuration uses a matrix of checkboxes where rows are 'from' statuses and columns are 'to' statuses. The only non-admin role available is 'Member' (there is no 'Developer' role). Custom fields of format 'list' store their options as CustomOption records. Status-based boards represent each status as a distinct column. The agent must create statuses before configuring workflows, and the custom field before creating the work packages.
